Signs & Murals
Hand-lettered · Gold Leaf
Architectural Murals

A Dying Craft, Kept Alive

Sign Painter.
Nearly 50 Years
of the Brush.

Long before "hand-lettering" became a trend, Chris Krieg was grinding his own pigments, pulling striping quills, and gilding glass in the classical tradition. He has worked as a professional sign painter and muralist for nearly five decades — a living link to a craft that once lined every main street in America.

His tools are the same ones the old-timers used: oil-based enamels, Japan colors, natural hair brushes, real gold leaf, and copal varnishes. No vinyl. No shortcuts.

For 40 years that work has been rooted in Evergreen, Colorado — painted across storefronts, vehicles, and walls throughout the Front Range and the mountain communities.

50
Years
of Paint
“

The brush doesn't lie. Every stroke either works or it doesn't. Fifty years in, that's still the thing that gets me out of bed.

— Chris Krieg, Evergreen CO

Medium & Method

Classical Materials,
No Compromise

Chris works exclusively with oil paints — ground pigments suspended in linseed, walnut, and stand oils — the same medium masters have used for five centuries. Layers are built slowly, dried properly, and sealed with traditional copal and damar varnishes that deepen color and protect the surface for generations.

In his sign and mural work, the same philosophy applies: oil enamels, natural hair lettering brushes, real gold and silver leaf applied with oil size. Every project is done the right way, the old way.

Oils Damar Varnish Copal Varnish Linseed Oil Japan Colors Gold Leaf Natural Hair Brushes Oil Enamels

Get in Touch

Commission Original Work

Paintings, murals, signs, and gold leaf work. Chris takes on a limited number of commissions each year and works with collectors and businesses throughout Colorado and beyond. Studio based in Evergreen, CO — the mountains are always open.
